title: The One With The Male Nanny (200th episode)

lines.speaker: Russian to Roman alphabet
lines.text: Gabriela Horber
Dedicated to the great work of Eric Aasen, Guineapig and many, many more
Chandler's hotel room in Tulsa. He's fast asleep when the telephone rings.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (picking up the telephone, answering it with a frog in his throat) Hello? (he clears his throat, but he still has the same frog in his throat when he speaks again) Hello?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (in her apartment, screaming) I LOVE MY NEW JOB!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honey, you're screaming.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: YOU BET YOUR ASS I AM! I just had the best first day ever! The kitchen: twice as big as Allessandro's.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (yawning) Oh, that's great.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, a-a-and clean. Not just health department clean... Monica clean.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (clearly not so interested) Awesome.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, and the people are so nice. There's this one guy, Geoffrey, he's the Maitre D., Chandler, you will love him. He is without a doubt, the funniest guy I have ever met. (Chandler, who was almost asleep again, sits up straight in bed in an instant and can't believe what he just heard.)
OPENING CREDITS
Central Perk. Phoebe and Mike are on the couch, holding hands, while Phoebe puts milk in her coffee.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: This is nice.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know! (Phoebe picks up a little packet of sugar, shakes it, and then realises she can't open it with one hand, but doesn't want to let go of Mike's hand. She tries to tear open the packet with one hand.)

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: You need both hands for that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I kinda do. (Mike lets go of her hand) Well, how's this? (she takes Mike's hand and puts it on her breast, she tears open the sugar and puts it in her coffee. Mike thinks it over and nods appreciative. After that, they hold hands again. Joey now enters, and sees the two sitting on the couch, holding hands.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (in a very aaaaahhhhh sweeeeeet voice) Aaahhh, look at you two... holding hands... huh is this getting serious? (Phoebe and Mike, embarrassed, start babbling and look away.) Have you not talked about it yet? (They say nothing now, but smile) Am I making you uncomfortable? (smiles are becoming forced now, and he speaks to Mike) If you were bigger you'd hit me, huh...? Aaaaaahhhhhh (he turns away to the bar)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry... I'm sorry. It's obviously way too early for us to be... having that conversation.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (in a flash she answers) Maybe not, is it?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Okay, when I got divorced, I didn't think I'd feel this way about someone for a really long time... Then again, I didn't think I'd meet someone like you... and... this may be crazy soon, but... I want you to have this... (He tries to get something from his pocket, but it's not that easy... Phoebe looks in a "what's happening" face to him. He finally has found something) No, not... that's gum. (He digs in a little more.) Ooh, five bucks... I love it when that happens, you know... Think no note's there...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (impatient) I know Mike, why don't you keep digging?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Oh, sorry. (He digs in again and finally finds what he's been looking for. A key.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it's a key. To be honest, I think I'd prefer the five dollars.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: It's to my apartment.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (really surprised) Oh wow, ooh! Ooh, big step for Phoebe and Mike.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah, look, and I don't want you to feel like you have to give me your key just because...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, I want to.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Oh, thank God. (he laughs nervously, and Phoebe gets her keychain from her bag.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah... ooh... wow... Even started to think I'd never meet someone that, you know, I wanted to... do this with. Here you go. (Both Phoebe and Mike look really proud.)

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Is this cool, huh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It really is.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sitting at his table) Oh, I know it... It is amazing these little things open doors... huh! (mimes opening a door with his own keys, Phoebe looks at him in a "yeah, yeah, yeah" way.)
Ross and Rachel's apartment. Both of them sitting on the couch, interviewing a nanny candidate.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So I don't go back to work for another four weeks, but we would like our nanny to start right away, so that Emma could get a chance to know her.

lines.speaker: Prospective nanny
lines.text: (in a sweet, caring voice) I think that's really smart. The easier we can make the transition for her, the better. (Ross and Rachel seem pleased with the answer.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's great, great. So do you have any questions for us?

lines.speaker: Prospective nanny
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Allright. Well thank you so much for coming... (they're standing up and make their way to the door)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really nice to meet you... and we'll call you.

lines.speaker: Prospective nanny
lines.text: Oh, you know, wait. I do have one question. (she starts playing with her hair) Do you guys do random drug testing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Boy, we uhm... hadn't really thought of that.

lines.speaker: Prospective nanny
lines.text: That's cool. But... but if you do, I'm gonna need three days notice.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okidoki! (and she slams the door in the nanny's face while Ross crumples up the application form) Wow! We're never gonna find a nanny.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, come on Rach, we will. I promise. We have more interviews (They sit down and Rachel sighs) And worse comes to worse, we can always reconsider the uhm... the first one we met with.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (indignant) What, the blonde with no bra?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She was blonde? (he looks surprised for a while, but then gets a "gotcha" expression on his face... There's knocking at the door.) Just a sec.! Okay, okay. This one's name is Sandy. She's got a degree in early childhood education, uhm... she worked for her last family for three years.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay... (Ross opens the door.)

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: (a guy) Hi... I'm Sandy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And she's a little mannish...
Phoebe's apartment. There's a knock on the door, and Phoebe opens it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (gasps) Oh my God! David!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Hi! I-i-is this a bad time?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (excited) No! It's a great time, come in...! WOW, hi... Oh my gosh! What are you doing here? Are you back from Minsk?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Well, just for a couple of days, uhm... I'm here to explain to the people who gave us our grant, why it's a positive thing that we spent all their money and uhm... accomplished uhm... nothing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Who cares, it got you here.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Well, it got me to New York anyway, and then I got on a cab at the airport, and the guy said where to? and I just... gave him your address I... I... I didn't even think about it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow. Where is your luggage? (David thinks for a while)

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Damn it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: A-Allright, well... I'll call the cab company.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Wa... wa... wait! We can... call them later. Can you just... just stand there f-f-for a moment? Boy! There's an old Russian expression, uhm... it goes: Schto ya ztez vigul... ui! Roughly translated that means uhm... This thing that I'm looking at: wow!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you! God, no! You should see me when... Oh actually, no, I look pretty good.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Are... are you kidding? You know, when you don't see someone for a long time, a-a-and you kind of build them up in your head and you start thinking about: Come on, don't be crazy. Nobody is that beautiful, but... well, you are. (Phoebe seems very charmed) Well, so, uhm... are you seeing... anyone? (Phoebe is still up on a cloud from what David just said)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No...
Monica and Chandler's apartment. Monica sits on the couch and Phoebe is pacing up and down the room.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm... I'm just... I'm the worst person ever. How can I not tell David that I'm seeing Mike?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Maybe he didn't give you a chance.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He said: Are you seeing someone? And I said no...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, well... That had been your window.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! I mean, I don't know. I was just , I was looking, I was looking in his eyes and I was just thinking: Oh my God! It's David. David's here. He's just, he's so irresistible.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really? The scientist guy?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really? Chandler?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Continue...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.Okay, then it gets worse, 'cause then I told him that I would see him tomorrow night.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know! (points at herself) Evil! And... and... and... I like Mike so much, you know. It's just going really well. Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow, isn't it ironic that David would show up on the same day that you and Mike exchange keys?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (sarcastically) Uhuh... Yeah...!, you know. And given my life long search for irony, you can imagine how happy I am.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you gonna do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I mean I guess, I just have to... tell David that nothing can happen between us. Unless I don't... You know, complicated moral situation, no right, no wrong...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You have to tell David!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I knew I should have had this conversation with Joey.
The hallway between the two apartments. Chandler comes home.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (angry) Funniest guy she's ever met! (to the door) I'm funny, right...? What do you know, you're a door... You just like knock-knock jokes... (laughs about himself, but then gets determined again) Save it for inside! (he enters)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Heeeeeey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So... Oklahoma is a crazy place. You know, they call it the Sooner state. Frankly I'd sooner be in any other state. (Monica looks at Phoebe, who also doesn't know what to say.) And what's with Oklahoma having a pan handle? Can all states have stuff like that? Hey yeah, I'm from the waistband, Wyoming. But when I was seven, we headed over to the crotch.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Doesn't believe what she's hearing) Was your cabin pressurised?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (laughs, but then moves to Phoebe) And don't get me started on the way that people from Tulsa talk.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (and she walks away)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What's with the word y'all? You know, just... two words just... pushed together... Are we all allowed to do that, because if so, I say why stop there? You know, your new poodle could be your noodle. And fried chicken? Could be fricken.Waiter, waiter excuse me, I'll have the fricken? (Monica laughs) See, that's... that's funny with the fricken, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, it just remind me of something this guy did today at work. I told you about that funny guy, Geoffrey, right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, he came up...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, he did this bit... You probably had to be there, but it was Liza Minelli locked in our freezer, eating a raw chicken. (Phoebe bursts out in a laugh)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Were you there?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, but it sounds like it was fricken funny...
Ross and Rachel's apartment. They're interviewing Sandy.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: I really do understand how hard it's gotta be to leave your child with another person. I mean, it's leaving behind a piece of your heart... (Ross has got that bored/angry/skeptic look and Rachel is very emotional)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sandy, that's exactly what it is...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you gay? (Rachel turns to Ross in an embarrassed way)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross!

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: It's okay. I get that a lot doing what I do. But I am straight. I-I'm engaged actually.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Her name is Deliah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that's pretty.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (skeptic) So you're just like a... guy who's a nanny?

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: I realise how it's... a bit unorthodox for some people, but I really believe, the most satisfying thing you can do with your life, is take care of a child.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (on a yeah, right tone) Okay.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Like in my last job, I met Daniel when he was three weeks old. And I got to watch him grow into this awesome person... When I left, I said: I'll see you soon... And he said to me: Skdandy... (Ross and Rachel look puzzled) That was his name for me... I'll see you every day... right in... (points at his heart, but starts to cry before he can finish his sentence. Rachel tries to comfort him, but Ross has this "you've got to be kidding me" look all over him)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, kids say all kinds of crap. (In the other room Emma starts to cry.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God, she mu... she must need her diaper changed.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Oh, oh, I can do it for her, if you want...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that would be great! (Sandy leaves for Emma's room) I love him, I love him, I love him...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, come on, Rach, he's a guy!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So wh..? He's smart, he's qualified. Give me one good reason we shouldn't try him out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Because, it's weird!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What kind of job is that for a man? A nanny? I-It's like if a woman wanted to be...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (she's got that "yeah, try to say it" look on her face) Yes?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: King?

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: I er... I hope you don't mind. I used some of my home-made lotion on Emma. It's a mixture of calendula and honey cream. It'll dry that rash right up. Plus... It keeps the hands young... (it makes Rachel smile)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (whispering and begging) Please? (Ross makes a "whatever" gesture) YES! Sandy you're hired.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: That's great! (He gets emotional again and waves his hand in front of his face in a feminine way, like trying to dry his tears) I'm sorry. It's just... such an emotional thing when you're welcomed into a new family...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oooh... ***I really can't hear what she says*** come here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You gotta be at least bi...
COMMERCIAL BREAK
Joey's apartment. Joey is reading a 'Busty Ladies' magazine when Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! (Joey doesn't look up, but gestures 'wait a minute' with his finger while he finishes reading the article. Chandler waits for Joey to finish.) I need you to set me up for a joke. Later, when Monica is around, I need you to ask me about fire trucks.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh. I-I don't know Chan. I'm not so good with remembering lines.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Can't believe what he hears) Well, thank God your livelihood doesn't depend on it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, right? (Chandler has really big eyes, and nods) Wh... Wh... Why are we doing this?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica says that her Maitre D. is the funniest guy she's ever met.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Seriously? She actually said that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! Am I crazy to be this upset?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nooooo! Being funny is your thing!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Without that, you just got "lame with women".

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ye.... (stops because he realises what Joey just said, and stares at him. At this moment Monica enters)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi! There you are.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sees Monica) Fire trucks! (Chandlers eyes double in size and he turns to Monica who doesn't understand what's happening. Then he turns back to Joey, who says "you're welcome" without a sound)
Phoebe's apartment. Phoebe just let David in who brought a bottle of wine.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Wow, you look even... more beautiful than you did yesterday.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oof...

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: In fact, ehm... I going to kiss you now. (David holds Phoebe and wants to kiss her.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, wait, wait!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yeah, I-I don't, I can't get away with stuff like that. I-I-It sounded sexy in my head, so I...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, it's not that. (they go sit on the couch) Uhm... Remember when you asked me if I was seeing someone and I said no? Well, uhm... I am. His ... his name is Mike.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Oh... oh...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I should have told you.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: No... well, yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Well, i-it's okay. I-I-I understand... Well, s... well, are you happy with this guy?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I am happy.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Damn it! I-I'm sorry. I-I don't mean that. I-I want you to be happy... But only with me. No, uhm... that's not fair. Uh, who cares, leave him!. Oh, I don't mean that. Yes I do... I'm sorry Uhm, I... I think I should probably uhm... go...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well... but David, just... I just want you to know that... that... you know... telling you this... is one of the hardest things I've ever had to do.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Well... just so you know... hearing it wasn't exactly a Vladnik carnival either... Can we at least hug goodbye?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Of course, yeah.(they hug and Phoebe sighs... a little after that also David sighs and makes his way to the door) You know, a kiss on the cheek wouldn't be totally inappropriate...

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: No... no...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I mean... (David kisses Phoebe on the cheek, makes his way to the door and turns around again)

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: In Minsk...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: ...it's uhm... i-it's two on each cheek and uhm... and one on the lips.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, if that's what they do in Minsk... (They kiss 2 on each cheek... and then they pause... David turns to the door) In New York... it's... (and then David grabs her by her neck and kisses her full on the lips... They kiss passionately and cannot seem to stop.)
Ross and Rachel's apartment. Rachel and Sandy sit on the couch. Sandy holds Emma. Ross enters the apartment. Sandy and Rachel wipe their tears away with handkerchiefs

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (in a tearful voice) Oh... Oh boy... (she turns around and sees Ross) Hi...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (very worried) Is everything allright?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, it's fine, it's fine. Sandy was just... was just telling me about how he proposed to his fiancee and it was just sooo beautiful.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Well, her favourite flower is the camellia. From the poem...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't... I can't hear it again.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: You know, I can't tell it again... (wipes his tears again)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And I'm fine never having heard it... (Rachel looks at Ross in a "why do you say that" manner) Rach, can I... can I see you for a sec?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! (to Sandy) Excuse me... (She walks to the kitchen with Ross and sighs)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Do you realise that man has cried in our apartment three times...? Huh? I haven't cried that many times since I moved in.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look, Ross, he's just... Sandy is just sensitive, that's all.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (picks up a cookie) Okay, okay, see... that... that is the problem. He is too sensitive. (takes a bite from the cookie)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What...? Too sensitive to take care of our baby?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (speaking with his mouth full) Yeah, I mean, all things that guy... (looks at the cookie) These are amazing!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sandy made Madeleines.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This... this is exactly what I'm talking about. What kind of a guy makes... makes... delicate French cookies, huh? They're not even... butch, manly cookies with... with... you know with... with chunks. (takes a careful bite from the cookie)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I... you know, I-I-I don't know what to say... I mean, I never thought of you as a guy who needed his men to be men. You know, 'cause I gotta tell you Ross, it not like you just came in from branding cattle.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey... there's sensitive... and there is too sensitive.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, what? What is too sensitive? (There's music coming from the living room. Ross opens the door to the living room and he and Rachel see Sandy play a song for Emma on his recorder. Rachel is moved by this, but Ross only sees his point proven again, and walks back into the kitchen, angrily. The door he was holding, swings back and hits Rachel.)
Phoebe's apartment. She and David are still kissing each other in the living room.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hmmm... No, no... No, I can't do this. It's bad.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: But... I-I-It's nice... A-a-and... nice is good. A-a-and good is not bad, ergo, w-w-we should keep kissing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no. No.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: But... ergo...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Look David, if... if you had never left, then... yeah, we'd probably still be together right now, but... you did leave, and I-I'm with Mike and I really care about him...

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Uhm... uhm... Goodbye... Uh... Schto ya ztez vigul... ui... (David holds his hands gently on the back of Phoebe's neck. There's a sound of a a bunch of keys rattling, and the door opens... It's Mike)

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Well... hey, the key works...! (he looks as if he doesn't want to believe what's happening)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (points to David) And you thanks for the face massage. Thank you.
Monica and Chandler's apartment. Monica is in the kitchen and Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did Rachel tell you we hired a male nanny?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! I think that's great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh really? Did she tell you he plays the recorder, recites poetry and bakes Madeleines?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh... How are they?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looks like in heaven) Lighter than air... (changes back to serious) But that's not the point. (Joey now also enters)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey...! Rachel and I hired a male nanny.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looks surprised) Really...? Guys do that...? That's... weird...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's like a woman wanting to be a...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...a what? A what? What's the end of that sentence?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes... What is the end of that sentence?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhm... A penis model. (Monica can't believe what's she's hearing and Ross pats Joey on the back.) Anyway, hey... Did you tell Chandler that some guy from work is the funniest guy you've ever met?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, so?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really? Do you not know Chandler?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is that why he's acting so weird...? He's jealous...? Oh my God, that is crazy. It's not like I'm attracted to Geoffrey...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So what? Being funny is Chandler's thing... You know, like Ross's thing is... (he can't come up with anything)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Science...? Academia...? Being a good father...?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: ...No... (he just can't seem to grasp it)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe he's that upset about this...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Monica, you have to do some damage control here, okay. 'Cause he's feeling like... (the door opens and Chandler walks in with a pizza)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Heeeyy! Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you guys talking about?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhm... Rachel and I hired a male nanny. (Joey makes a gesture and sound like "Can you believe that?")

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You got a man who's a nanny...? You got a manny...? (Monica starts laughing, but very exaggerated. Joey realises they also should laugh and punches Ross. Now all three of them laugh, but very fake. Chandler seems happy again.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, I don't mind a... male nanny, but I do draw the line at a male wetnurse. (again they laugh, even more fake than before)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohhh, ooohhh... you are on a roll, mister!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If I'd known you guys were coming over, I would have brought more pizza. (they all burst out in a thundering laugh)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, okay... Chandler you... you stop it! (Monica wipes away tears)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What is so funny about that? (they realise it wasn't a joke)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I don't know... I-It's... just the way you say it... I mean, you're funny... You have that funny thing. You're a funny guy! (Chandler turns to Joey)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did you tell her what we talked about? (Joey starts laughing hysterically, but then gets serious again...)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah....

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So those were pity laughs? PITY LAUGHS? (Joey and Ross walk away from the kitchen)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, listen... You have nothing to worry about with Geoffrey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah? Is he funnier than me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, you're... you're different funny... I mean, you're... you're more sarcastic a-a-and... well, he does... bits... and impressions... and... and limericks...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I do limericks... uhm... There once was a man named Chandler, whose wife made him die inside.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, you know I think you're hilarious! Come on, you know that joke you told me last week? The one about Jerry Lewis and the girl with the lazy eye...? (he laughs) That slayed me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey... I made up that joke and told it to you! (He points at Chandler. Joey gestures to Ross "What are you doing?)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Not knowing when to shut up...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yep! That's my thing...
COMMERCIAL BREAK
Phoebe's apartment. David is still there. Mike closes the door.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: So... how many guys have your key?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, no, no, no... It's not... it's not... i'ts not as bad as it looks... really. I was just saying goodbye to an old friend.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Your lipstick's on his mouth.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Oh, uh... we just uh... happen to wear the same shade.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, uhm... David and I did use to go out... but years ago, and he lives in Minsk. He's only... he's only in town for a couple of days.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Did you uhm...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no...

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: ...kiss him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, well, yeah...

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yes, but uhm... You should know... she really likes you. I-In fact I-I-I don't think you realise j-just how lucky you are fella. (he points at Mike)

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Don't point your finger at me.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Why? Wh-What are you going to do about it?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Well... I'll... just show you what I'm gonna do about it... (he hits David's finger with his finger and they start to finger-fight using their fingers as swords saying all kinds of macho crap)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Stop it! Stop it, before someone gets really hurt! (they stop and Phoebe gets David's jacket and gives it to him) Here David, you should just go.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Allright... But... if I ever do come back from Minsk... (points at Mike) well, you just better watch out.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Well, if I ever goto Minsk, you'd better watch out.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Oh, you're going to Minsk?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Well, I might.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Really? Well, if you do, come in the spring. It's just lovely there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, well... guys?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Right... Goodbye Phoebe. (Makes a move to kiss her.)

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Hey, what are you kidding me?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Right-o, right-o... (to Mike) Take good care of her. (and he leaves)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry, I'm so sorry. If you... If you want your key back, I totally understand.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: It's never gonna happen again right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Right! Never! Never! I swear! (They kiss... The door opens and David comes in again.)

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I-I... Oh I...I just wanna say uhm... if you do ever come to Minsk, that's my number (gives Mike a business card) We'll uhm... we'll party up Vladnik style. (He leaves again)
Ross walks in the hallway to his apartment and stops in front of his own door. Now he hears two recorders playing a song. When he enters, Sandy and Joey are playing the recorder and Rachel listens.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Allright! Hey, hey Ross. Check it out! Sandy taught me Hot-cross Buns.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really? Sounded like Three Blind Mice.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Noooo... Three Blind Mice goes like this... (he puts his fingers in position on the recorder)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looks angrily at Joey and points at him) I swear to God...! (Joey is in shock)

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Who's up for puppets?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me! I'm up for puppets!

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Well, please welcome... The Snufflebumps... Who wants to be mr. Wigglemunch and who's gonna be the Grumpus?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, okay... How exactly is a two month old supposed to appreciate puppets?

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Actually studies have shown that the movement and colours help their cerebral development... The whimsical characters are just for us. (He winks to Joey and Rachel. Ross's face says he disapproves. Joey sees that and kind of angrily says...)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I wanna be mr. Wigglemunch. (and makes a "there" nod to Ross)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (shakes his head) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Well, I guess we know who's gonna be the Grumpus... (Ross goes to the kitchen)
Ross and Rachel's kitchen. Ross got a beer from the refrigerator and opens the bottle. Rachel now also enters the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That was kind of rude!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry. Please apologise to Sandy and the Snufflebumps for me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know, he was just doing his job...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, you know what... I-I'm sorry I'm the only one who isn't in love with Gary Poppins out there... But I just... I can't... I can't go through with this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, come on Ross...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! Hey, you know what? I'm sorry. I would never force you... to hire someone you were this uncomfortable with...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sighs) Oh... That's true.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, you're the one who wants to fire him, so you're gonna have to do it. (Ross walks to the living room determined to fire Sandy)
Living room. Joey and Sandy are talking with the Snufflebumps.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: (In a puppet voice) So you see Wigglemunch, that's why it's important to shaaaaaaare...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (kind of emotional) I am learning so much from you.
Monica and Chandler's apartment. Monica and Joey are sitting at the dinner table. Chandler comes from the bedroom with his suitcase.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I'm off to Tulsa, so if your Maitre D. friend has any funny Oklahoma jokes, tell him to e-mail me at www.hahanotsomuch.com.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, you can relax. Last night at work, Geoffrey told this really sexist joke. After that, not so funny anymore.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really...? See... that's the thing: you gotta keep it smart, people!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, don't miss that flight. You know I love you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I love you too. (Monica and Chandler kiss. He turns to Joey.) And... I like you as a friend. (They hug and pat each other on the back.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Allright. See you later!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See ya! (he leaves the apartment)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Monica) Did that guy really make that joke?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Naaaa... He still kills me. Last night he had me laughing so hard, I swear... a little pee came out.
Ross and Rachel's apartment. Sandy is knitting baby clothes. Ross and Rachel walk into the living room.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Here goes...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't watch. It's like firing Elmo. (Ross walks to the couch where Sandy sits)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sandy... Hi, we uhm... we kinda need to talk. I'm afraid it's not working out.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: (surprised) Oh...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, uhm... I mean, Rachel and I, think you are great... with Emma... uhm... We just feel...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (from behind the bedroom door) YOU! You feel!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I... just feel that the... the chemistry isn't right. I'm sorry. We're... we're more than happy to give you good recommendation...

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Oh, no, no, no... That's okay. I got a lot of offers from other families. I just picked you guys because... I liked you the best.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (from bedroom) Oh, damn you Geller!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Anyway, uhm...Well, I'm glad there's no hard feelings.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: No, none at all. You need to be happy with whoever is in your home... Although if you don't mind telling me, what was your problem? Maybe it's something I can work on in the future.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, you know, it's uhm... nothing you did, it's... it's uhm... my issue.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: What is it...? (Ross hesitates) Please...? (he tilts his head)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know, I'm just not uhm... that comfortable with a guy who's as sensitive as you.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: That's fair... Although, can I ask... why do you think that is?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why... I... I don't know. (Sandy tilts his head again) Uhm... errrr... maybe... maybe because of my father?

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: hmmm... (and shakes his head)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I mean, uhm... you know when I was growing up he was kind of a tough guy... You know a-a-and as a kid I wasn't the athlete I am now.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (from the bedroom) Huh ha ha!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I play squash...! Anyway, uhm... I uhm... I always get the feeling he thought I was too sensitive.

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: That must have been hard.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It was hard... I remember... I was in my bedroom... playing with my dinosaurs... playing and learning... and my father walks in and says... he says... "What are you doing with those things? What's wrong with you, why aren't you... why aren't you outside playing like a... like a real boy?

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: But you are a real boy!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know I am! (Ross now starts to cry) ...And when it's summer, and it's hot, why can't you wear a tank top?

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: It's allright! Crying is good. It lets the boo-hoos out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Here come some more...
COMMERCIAL BREAK
Joey's apartment. Joey and Sandy are sitting at the kitchen counter. Joey is holding mr. Wigglemunch, and Sandy holds the Grumpus.

lines.speaker: Sandy/Grumpus
lines.text: And what's the one kind of boat that can never, ever sink?

lines.speaker: Joey/Wigglemunch
lines.text: What kind?

lines.speaker: Sandy/Grumpus
lines.text: A friend-ship...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! You blow my mind...

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Oh, I gotta go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aaahh... How much do I owe you?

lines.speaker: Sandy
lines.text: Twenty bucks.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's like the cheapest college ever.
END
